title: Area of rectangles
description: Find area of rectangles and squares by multiplying side lengths.
topic_path: Topic B: Concepts of area measurement

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following rectangles have an area of $12$ square meters?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The area of a rectangle can be found by multiplying its two side lengths.

These rectangles do not have an area of  $12$ square meters.

Figure | Area
:-: | :-:
[[☃ image 1]]|| $5 \text{ m} \times 3 \text{ m} = \purpleD{15 \text { square meters}}$
[[☃ image 2]]| | $4\text{ m} \times 2\text{ m} = \redE{8\text { square meters}}$

These rectangles have an area of  $12$ square meters.

Figure | Area
:-: | :-:
[[☃ image 1]]| $6\text{ m} \times 2 \text{ m} = \blueE{12\text { square meters}}$  
[[☃ image 2]] | $3\text{ m} \times 4 \text{ m} = \greenE{12\text { square meters}}$

choices.content: ![A rectangle with the shorter side labeled 2 meters and the longer side labeled 6 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c16b31e41b0c704afc8b95e7cdbe3948612e25ae)
![A rectangle with the shorter side labeled 3 meters and the longer side labeled 4 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4ee8a61770bb7a3d7fe18d5f482d82d34c05f08b)
![A rectangle with the left side labeled 3 meters and the top side labeled 5 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45d0502d001b256d254c5379d37d6807ebbe045b)
![A rectangle with the left side labeled 2 meters and the top side labeled 4 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/be65c1f3fdf10b2aa825108e3af2b02927b4c761)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following rectangles have an area of $16$ square meters?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The area of a rectangle can be found by multiplying its two side lengths.

These rectangles do not have an area of  $16$ square meters.

Figure | Area
:-: | :-:
[[☃ image 1]]| $5 \text{ m} \times 3 \text{ m} = \blueE{15 \text { square meters}}$  
[[☃ image 2]]| $10\text{ m}\times 6\text{ m} = \goldE{60\text{ square meters}}$

These rectangles have an area of  $16$ square meters.

Figure | Area
:-: | :-:
[[☃ image 1]] | $8 \text{ m} \times 2 \text{ m} = \greenE{16 \text { square meters}}$  
[[☃ image 2]]  | $4 \text{ m} \times 4 \text{ m} = \purpleD{16 \text { square meters}}$

choices.content: ![A rectangle with the shorter side labeled 3 meters and the longer side labeled 5 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a31da8e5fa3cbf885d7c0701f65f99aa11f6fd25)
![A rectangle with the left side labeled 2 meters and the top side labeled 8 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a6c9c35ba40847cb8b59c429a750a5860cd0a8d0)
![A rectangle with the left side labeled 4 meters and the top side labeled 4 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4c61d21e5190147ad8b9541fd9a78c0ffc001870)
![A rectangle with the shorter side labeled 6 meters and the longer side labeled 10 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/846981e9a41918b9e27c346a869a61cab7cd5baf)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following rectangles have an area of $20$ square meters?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The area of a rectangle can be found by multiplying its two side lengths.

These rectangles do not have an area of  $20$ square meters.

Figure | Area
:-: | :-:
[[☃ image 1]]  | $4\text{ m} \times 4\text{ m} = \purpleD{16\text { square meters}}$  
[[☃ image 2]] | $7\text{ m} \times 3\text{ m} = \redE{21\text { square meters}}$

These rectangles have an area of  $20$ square meters.

Figure | Area
:-: | :-:
[[☃ image 1]]| $5\text{ m} \times 4\text{ m} = \blueE{20\text { square meters}}$  
[[☃ image 2]]| $10\text{ m} \times 2\text{ m} = \greenE{20\text { square meters}}$

choices.content: ![A rectangle with the shorter side labeled 4 meters and the longer side labeled 5 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/80f2ec888d0775738d2ab47758cea83e41185f2e)
![A rectangle with the left side labeled 2 meters and the top side labeled 10 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aed67bf29c9bbdcc6b6cb90260e8f1fd994c8ebb)
![A rectangle with one side labeled 4 meters and another side also labeled 4 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0afae8d03634560f15077f6264e8b16570b0e8a2)
![A rectangle with the shorter side labeled 3 meters and the longer side labeled 7 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5b3f243e722b50034c677929888c5e72a70c7092)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following rectangles have an area of $24$ square meters?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The area of a rectangle can be found by multiplying its two side lengths.

These rectangles do not have an area of  $24$ square meters.

Figure | Area
:-: | :-:
[[☃ image 1]]| $5\text{ m} \times 5\text{ m} = \blueE{25\text { square meters}}$
[[☃ image 2]]| $4\text{ m} \times 20\text{ m} = \goldE{80\text { square meters}}$

These rectangles have an area of  $24$ square meters.

Figure | Area
:-: | :-:
[[☃ image 1]] | $8\text{ m} \times 3\text{ m} = \greenE{24\text { square meters}}$  
[[☃ image 2]] | $4\text{ m} \times 6\text{ m} = \purpleD{24\text { square meters}}$

choices.content: ![A rectangle with the left side labeled 5 meters and the top side labeled 5 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c6ad246254d3cc5f8f784aeaf985c57a06c46f6a)
![A rectangle with the left side labeled 3 meters and the top side labeled 8 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/590a17b0b8ab0e746b74021ded1362d593504b85)
![A rectangle with the shorter side labeled 4 meters and the longer side labeled 6 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8308064ca6bc2f8bee18344e0f0819b28f58ba3c)
![A rectangle with the shorter side labeled 4 meters and the longer side labeled 20 meters.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6b48bdec6ef9735cc915c38d4bd311fb00c9b00e)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following rectangles have an area of $36$ square meters?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The area of a rectangle can be found by multiplying its two side lengths.

These rectangles do not have an area of  $36$ square meters.

Figure | Area
:-: | :-:
[[☃ image 1]] | $7\text{ m} \times 5\text{ m} = \greenD{35\text { square meters}}$
[[☃ image 2]] |$8\text{ m} \times 4\text{ m} = \goldE{32\text { square meters}}$

These rectangles have an area of  $36$ square meters.

Figure | Area
:-: | :-:
[[☃ image 1]] | $9\text{ m} \times 4\text{ m} = \blueE{36\text { square meters}}$  
[[☃ image 2]] | $6\text{ m} \times 6\text{ m} = \purpleD{36\text { square meters}}$
